description: Discoverable bootstrap for bots and agents working in the AINL/OpenClaw repo. Pointers only; not an execution engine.

ainl_install_openclaw.description: One-command OpenClaw + AINL setup: merges env.shellEnv into openclaw.json (direct JSON — CLI rejects unknown keys), bootstraps SQLite weekly_remaining_v1, registers three gold-standard crons, prints health table

ainl_status.description: Unified health view: workspace, schema bootstrap, weekly budget (legacy weekly_remaining_v1 row preferred; memory_records aggregate fallback), cron presence, drift signal, 7-day token totals, estimated cost avoided in USD, env caps, overall health. summary_line in JSON; --summary for one-line Telegram/Slack; --json-summary for one-line CI JSON

ainl_cron_add.command: ainl cron add PATH.ainl (--cron 'EXPR' | --every 15m) [--name NAME] [--agent ID] [--session isolated] [--announce] [--dry-run]
ainl_cron_add.description: Wraps openclaw cron add: schedules ainl run <absolute-path> without crafting cron JSON manually

ainl_dashboard.description: Runs scripts/serve_dashboard.py (emitted server under tests/emits/server; build first with run_tests_and_emit if needed); opens browser unless --no-browser

ainl_doctor_ainl.description: OpenClaw + AINL integration validator: checks env vars, SQLite schema, cron names, OPENCLAW_BOOTSTRAP_PREFER_SESSION_CONTEXT; prints fix hints and openclaw_ainl_fix_suggestions copy-paste commands

ainl_doctor.description: General environment diagnostics: Python/import health, PATH checks, MCP config validation (recognizes OpenClaw JSON, ZeroClaw JSON, Hermes YAML automatically)

ainl_install_mcp.description: Bootstrap MCP wiring for a host: merges mcpServers.ainl into host config, installs ainl-run, PATH hint. Aliases: ainl install-openclaw, ainl install-zeroclaw, ainl hermes-install

setup_script.description: Shell convenience wrapper around `ainl install openclaw`. Supports --dry-run to preview without changes. Now delegates to ainl CLI (no longer uses deprecated gateway config.patch). v1.3.0+.

required_steps: Confirm the task is not already implemented
Confirm relevant adapter/function semantics from code, not memory
Explicitly state any assumption that could break the plan
Avoid inferring payload access from list/enumeration APIs unless code proves it
Provide a validation plan before coding
Choose a different task if the chosen one is already implemented
